name: Amazon Lightsail
what_it_does: AWS's simplified VPS platform with fixed-price instances, managed databases, and simplified networking for small to medium workloads.
use_cases: Deploy simple agent backends on fixed-price instances without AWS complexity
Host small web applications with predictable monthly costs using Lightsail's flat-rate pricing
Run containerized workloads using Lightsail Container Services without ECS/EKS overhead
Set up managed PostgreSQL or MySQL databases with point-in-time backups at low cost
Create static website hosting with CDN distribution as a simplified CloudFront alternative
not_for: High-scale workloads needing Auto Scaling, complex VPC configurations, or enterprise AWS services
Teams already on full AWS who benefit from native EC2/RDS integration
Microservices architectures requiring service mesh, advanced networking, or K8s
best_when: Best for simple, predictable workloads where Lightsail's fixed pricing and simplicity beats raw AWS complexity.
avoid_when: Avoid for workloads that need to scale beyond Lightsail's instance sizes or integrate deeply with other AWS services.

auth.notes: AWS SigV4 signing via boto3/AWS SDK. Dedicated IAM permissions for Lightsail resources.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 3 months free trial on select instance plans for new AWS customers
pricing.paid_tiers: From $3.50/mo for 512MB RAM instance to $160/mo for 32GB RAM; managed DB from $15/mo
pricing.notes: Fixed predictable monthly pricing — no per-request billing. Data transfer overages apply.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limited per account; Lightsail API is less rate-limit-documented than core AWS APIs

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AWS SDK error format with code and message; Lightsail-specific errors can be sparse.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Instance creation is not idempotent — must check existence before creating to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Lightsail instances are in a separate network from VPC resources — connecting to EC2/RDS requires VPC peering setup
Lightsail API is separate from core AWS APIs and uses its own endpoint (lightsail.us-east-1.amazonaws.com) not the standard regional endpoint
Static IP addresses are billed separately — releasing an attached static IP while instance is stopped incurs charges
Lightsail Container Services use different auth than EC2 — push images via lightsail push-container-image CLI command
Managed database backup window and maintenance window cannot overlap — misconfiguring causes maintenance to be skipped

security_score_components.security_notes: IAM-controlled access. Lightsail instances have a simplified firewall separate from Security Groups.
